data.text/html:
Q4 · Person Lens — Dataset Comparison

Each chart reacts to the selected member. The key finding is that the bias between datasets is not in how events were scored, but in who was included and which travel records were kept.
  • (a) TROUT-accused members: select Teddy Goldstein or Ed Helpsford and observe how FILAH excluded both entirely
  • (b) Missing evidence: Figs B and E quantify suppressed government-zone travel — the main omission
  • (c) Most FILAH-distorted: Fig D ranks all members by coverage; the red card above names the answer
  • (d) FILAH bias: Fig C shows the commercial-only narrative FILAH constructs by omitting government travel

A · Participation Record Coverage

Participation records per dataset. Zero bar = person fully excluded from meeting record.

B · Zone Travel Volume (sub-question b)

Absolute waypoints per zone. FILAH records almost zero government-zone travel; journalist shows hundreds — the suppressed evidence.

C · Zone Framing (sub-question d)

Proportional view. FILAH bars are almost entirely commercial, hiding the government-zone majority that the journalist dataset reveals.

E · Missing Evidence by Zone (sub-question b)

Journalist waypoints absent from FILAH and TROUT. The government bar is the tallest — the largest gap between what was reported and the full record.

D · Coverage Scorecard — All Members (sub-question c)

% of journalist participation records captured per member. 0% = fully excluded. White bar = currently selected member. Members absent from a dataset appear at 0% for that dataset.
